TimeEntry.Notes: As checked and found in the myRMM portal patch management windows were not created for TALY and updates are necessary. Security Updates: These patches often contain vital security updates that protect

TimeEntry.Title: Onboarding

TimeEntry.Title: Onboarding
TimeEntry.Notes: At Tally, resetting QNAP Password and scanning the network to map the current topology

TimeEntry.Title: Onboarding
TimeEntry.Notes: Installing MPC box at TALY and putting the Winedge in service, Sophos is configured and will be put into production over the weekend to ensurethe work process is not interrupted. Created admin account

TimeEntry.Title: QNAP Upgrade for TALY QNAP

TimeEntry.Notes: Setup QNAP Sync to DC, Created the LUN and verified the folder structure on the source QNAPand started the sync.

TimeEntry.Notes: Fixed the access issue to Shared Folder reported by Rob.

TimeEntry.Title: TALY-HQ-WINEDGE : cisco umbrella manually updated with Cisco Secure Client Version: 5.1.10.233
TimeEntry.Notes: TALY-HQ-WINEDGE : cisco umbrella manually updated with Cisco Secure Client Version: 5.1.10.233
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: TALY-WIN-3V0EKA0U8E8 : cisco umbrella manually updated with Cisco Secure Client Version: 5.1.10.233
TimeEntry.Notes: TALY-WIN-3V0EKA0U8E8 : cisco umbrella manually updated with Cisco Secure Client Version: 5.1.10.233
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: TALY-HQ-WINEDGE : System vulnerability found as cisco umbrella not updated
TimeEntry.Notes: Accessed the system backend to perform the Cisco Umbrella update, which was completed successfully with latest version 5.1.12.146.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Internet down
TimeEntry.Notes: Customer experienced some outage earlier in the day when their systems rebooted. Everything came back online except internet. They contacted AT&T who told them that they needed to update the IP Address on their firewall. Checked the Unifi portal, but could not communicate with the device because it was offline. Went onsite to physically connect to the Unifi and updated the IP address per AT&T. Rebooted and verified that internet access was restored.

TimeEntry.Title: Internet going out intermittently
TimeEntry.Notes: Internet connection keeps dropping. Will not stay live for more than a couple of minutes. Check firewall settings and everything seems correct. Restored firewall backup taken a week ago and found that this fixed the issue. Tested for several minutes with continuous ping with no drops. Rob tested for a few minutes and confirmed that it is working much better than earlier.

TimeEntry.Title: QNAP firmware 5.2.7.3297 upgrade for TALY
TimeEntry.Notes: I logged into the QNAP system in the TALY environment and found that firmware updates were pending. Before proceeding with the update, Since a reboot was required prior to installing the firmware, I rebooted the QNAP device, installed the updates, and then performed a second reboot to finalize the installation. After the system came back online, I verified that all shared folders were accessible. the firmware upgrade completed successfully.

TimeEntry.Title: WIN-3V0EKA0U8E8 : Cisco umbrella update with latest version 5.1.12.146
TimeEntry.Notes: WIN-3V0EKA0U8E8 : Cisco umbrella updated with latest version 5.1.12.146 successfully.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Internet is not working. AT&T advised they check on router port 4
TimeEntry.Notes: Verified that Internet working from AT&T router and problem was on Unifi firewall. Saw that internet connection was plugged into SPF port 9 instead of the "internet" port on the firewall. Moved the cable over and rebooted the firewall. Verified that internet came back online. Jocelyn reported that internet seemed throttled when connected to their main WiFi ssid. Saw that 2.4Ghz was enabled but 5GHz was disabled. Enabled the 5GHz and that doubled their speed when connected from wifi on her mac. Provided recommendation for replacement battery for UPS.

TimeEntry.Title: Robert reported that Vickie could not access shares from outside the office
TimeEntry.Notes: Investigated issue remotely and found that Vickie's PC could not be reached. Went onsite and found that the IP address that was mapped on Vickie's mac was different from the current public IP. Updated the IP Address on the mac, updated the firewall rule to include both ports since we moved the cable last time, installed MyRemote on Vickie's PC. Fixed shared drive access for Robert and Benjamin as well. Discussed Filemaker issue with Jocelyn and found that the IP she gave me did not match her Mac. Will update that once I get back to the office.

TimeEntry.Title: Filemaker Access externally for Jocelyn
TimeEntry.Notes: Update firewall rules from 192.168.1.40 to 192.168.1.47. Jocelyn will test over the weekend.

TimeEntry.Title: Client visit
TimeEntry.Notes: Update firewall rules from 192.168.1.40 to 192.168.1.47. Created individual id and password to access

TimeEntry.Title: Help Vickie with Shared Folder access
TimeEntry.Notes: Connected to Vickie's Mac and verified that she was able to connect to the shared folder while from home. Tested several times to verify that it works. Not sure why it failed for her earlier, could not reproduce problem.

TimeEntry.Title: Fix storage and backups
TimeEntry.Notes: Worked on qnap, found quota issue. then worked on some issues vicky was having. Finally gave instructions to setup time machine properly on qnap and get the backups of the workstations done by india by giving instructions to the users on how to setup the backups.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Talley and Associates
TimeEntry.Notes:
Called Robert Evan regarding the Installation of tools on his System...

Got the remote access but didn;t  get the full access of the MAc computer....

Tried multiple step to set-up MyRemote agent....However everytime does not get the full access of the system..

So Robert to provide any other user name to call and set-up security tools...

So called Benjamin user.....he also has mac computer...so took the remote access and install securirty tool..

User already has older version of crowdstrike. so uninsstall the older version and install the latest version...

Also verified the tools on all the portal...

Same informed to robert that Benjamin system has been onboarded and tools has been informed...

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Talley and Associates
TimeEntry.Notes: As Ravi was onsite at Talley, he asked me to log into the QNAP and verify the quota for each user. After reviewing, he instructed me to increase the quotas. Later, after testing something with Robert, he directed me to remove the quota limits entirely. Following that, he requested that I set up Time Machine backups for the users on the QNAP. I attempted to contact the user to configure this, but the machine was not accessible. We then called Robert, gained access to one machine via MyRemote, reset the user’s password, and completed the Time Machine setup. Afterward, I worked with Surinder regarding tool installation and MyRemote access from Robert’s machine, but we were unsuccessful."

TimeEntry.Title: HBS3 sync issue to DC for Taly QNAP.
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the QNAP in DC to verify the backup syncing status from Taly. It was on error state regarding the connection error. Logged into Taly QNAP at their location and then troubleshoot the issue regarding communication between RTRR server settings. Find the error and then apply fix which helps the issues resolution. After that test the connection and stat syncing again. The has been synced again properly to DC QNAP.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Talley and Associates
TimeEntry.Notes: I called Robert Evans regarding the installation of tools on their systems. Christina Gomez answered the call and confirmed she uses a Mac computer. I took remote access and installed the security tool. I also verified all users with her; she mentioned there are only five users, with one currently on leave and expected back on Monday. Additionally, I set up Time Machine backup for her machine, created a user account on QNAP, and provided access to the designated folder.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Talley and Associates
TimeEntry.Notes:
Called the user Benjamin as need to set-up the Time Machine back-up and Also set-up the MyRemote again as his machine is showing offline in MyRemote...

Created the new session again and configure the TimeMachine Backup....

Also removed the old myremote agent and install the Myremote again and now the system is keep showing online in myremote....

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Talley and Associates
TimeEntry.Notes:
As per Ravi’s instructions, I tried to call the users for tool and backup setup, but both were not available, so the tasks have been scheduled for Monday morning.
He then instructed me to verify the QNAP storage space and configure Time Machine backup retention to prevent the QNAP from filling up. After researching retention settings, I found that while retention can be managed through macOS, it should ideally be handled via QNAP. We can use a single shared folder for all users, but Ravi requested creating individual users and folders for Time Machine. I attempted this, but QNAP does not provide that option.
After further research, no alternative option was found. I confirmed the available space with him: 4 TB free out of a 7 TB disk.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Talley and Associates
TimeEntry.Notes:
I called QNAP support and explained the issue during a remote session. We attempted to set up the configuration, and as per their suggestion, the best way to restrict Time Machine usage for each user is to create individual thick volumes for each user and then set their Time Machine backups into a shared folder within that volume. This approach allows setting a specific size for each user’s backup and prevents exceeding that limit without dealing with global quotas for all users.
Following their recommendation, I created a volume for each username with 750 GB allocated, then created a shared folder within that volume, set it up for Time Machine, and created the user with access to that folder. I then configured the Time Machine backup for Christina-TM and removed her access from the old shared folder for time machine backup.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Talley and Associates
TimeEntry.Notes:
Called Jocelyn for the installation of Security tools and Set-up time machine back-up....

So connect to the user system but didn't get the full access of the Mac system...

Guided the user to enable the ConnectWise agent under security and privacy and Screen Recording but after enabling still not getting the Full access...

So guided the user to remove the application and reboot the system...user did but still not getting the full acess...

So tried multiple times and got the full access and install ManageEngine MyRemote and Huntress on the system.

However again lost the Access...User said she has some urgent work and need the system..user said to call backa at 3pm...

Called the user at 3Pm again but user said she is still busy on project and aksed for a call back tomorrow at 3pm...

So will call the user tomorrow....

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Talley and Associates
TimeEntry.Notes: As per the recommendation from support, the best way to restrict Time Machine usage for each user is to create individual thick volumes for each user and then set their Time Machine backups into a shared folder within that volume. This approach allows us to allocate a specific size for each user’s backup and prevents exceeding that limit without relying on global quotas. Following this guidance, I created a separate volume for each username with 750 GB allocated, then created a shared folder within that volume, configured it for Time Machine, and assigned the user access to that folder. I then set up the Time Machine backup for Christina-TM and removed her access from the old shared folder previously used for backups. After completing these steps, I attempted to contact the user to assist with configuring their setup using the new folders and usernames and to confirm the removal of the old Time Machine folder.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Talley and Associates
TimeEntry.Notes:
Called Jocelyn regarding the tools installation and backup set-up.....

She said they are busy and also asked if she can transfer to Christina or Benjamin......She said that they are about to leave...

so asked for a call back on Monday at 1PM

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Talley and Associates
TimeEntry.Notes:
as Robert shared the login details Thank you for sharing the Porkbun login credentials — I’ve logged in and verified the account successfully. I also attempted to add the required DMARC DNS records from Porkbun. During this process, I confirmed that while talleyassoc.com is registered with Porkbun, the domain is not using Porkbun’s nameservers.

Based on a WHOIS and nameserver check, the domain is currently using the following authoritative nameservers:

Ns1 - 4.hostinger.com

Because DNS is hosted at Hostinger, any DNS records (including DMARC) must be added in Hostinger’s DNS zone. Records added in Porkbun will not be published or detected.

Next step:

 Please provide the Hostinger login credentials so we can add the DMARC record directly in Hostinger on your behalf and complete the verification.

 Once access is provided, we’ll take care of the rest. Robert request to send the details to Jocelyn so sent the details to her but didn't get any response.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Talley and Associates
TimeEntry.Notes: tried to setup the time machine backup for Jocelyn-TM on Carlos-iMac-2019 so facing the issue so removed the old entries and then rebooted the mac and setup the folder again and setup the time machine backup for the user.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Talley and Associates
TimeEntry.Notes: tried to setup the time machine backup for Benjamin-TM on Benjamins-MacBook-Air so facing the issue so removed the old entries and then rebooted the mac and setup the folder again and setup the time machine backup for the user.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Talley and Associates
TimeEntry.Notes: as followup with Robert regarding the EasyDMRC so got the credentials and tried to setup but takes long time to verify the domain so BIMI and SPF verification is pending and handed it over to next shift.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Talley and Associates
TimeEntry.Notes: as per instructions from Ravi setup and configure the inky for the client and its showing up but all the information is not popup like other clients.

TimeEntry.Title: TALY Monthly Site/Network/ Security scan - Jan 2026
TimeEntry.Notes: As per the monthly task, verified network detective scan reports for the Taly client and downloaded and verified the reports but the O365 reports were having the issues so troubleshooted and setup it again and started the sync and waiting for the reports.

TimeEntry.Title: TALY Monthly Site/Network/ Security scan - Jan 2026
TimeEntry.Notes: generated the reports for TALY and verified them and uploaded in their team channel.

TimeEntry.Title: QNAP firmware 5.2.8.3359 upgrade for TALY
TimeEntry.Notes: I logged into the QNAP system in the TALY environment and found that firmware updates were pending. Before proceeding with the update, I verified that all backup jobs had completed successfully. Since a reboot was required prior to installing the firmware, I rebooted the QNAP device, installed the updates, and then performed a second reboot to finalise the installation. After the system came back online, I verified that all shared folders were accessible. I then re-scanned the backup repository, and the scan completed successfully.

TimeEntry.Title: Referral to Wes Nichols by Investment Property Group
TimeEntry.Notes: As per Robert’s email, logged into the Microsoft 365 and EasyDMARC portals to check the SPF record. To access the domain hosting portal (Porkbun), an OTP was required as MFA was configured on info@talleyassoc.com. I emailed Robert to request the OTP. After receiving the OTP, logged into the portal and updated the DNS record. As per the portal, the changes will take some time to reflect. Since the update has been completed and is pending propagation, I informed Robert and assigned the ticket to the next shift for monitoring.

TimeEntry.Title: TALY Servers pending for reboot & updates after APD
TimeEntry.Notes:
Worked on the  ticket. Login to the manage engine portal and checked failed or missing patches  for the below mentioned machine then download and installed

the failed patches manually which are applicable to the computer. Then reboot the machine and  scan the machine for installed patches. TALY-HQ-WINEDGE

After scan Check and verify the machine with the MY RMM portal. The failed patches are updated successfully and machine are in healthy state.

TimeEntry.Title: Referral to Wes Nichols by Investment Property Group
TimeEntry.Notes: Sent an followup email to the user.

TimeEntry.Title: Referral to Wes Nichols by Investment Property Group
TimeEntry.Notes: Sent followup emails. No reply from the user. closing the ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: TALY Monthly Site/Network/ Security scan - Feb 2026
TimeEntry.Notes: As per the monthly task, verified network detective scan reports for the Taly client and downloaded and verified the reports but the O365 reports were having the issues so troubleshooted and setup it again and started the sync and waiting for the reports.

TimeEntry.Title: TALY Monthly Site/Network/ Security scan - Feb 2026
TimeEntry.Notes: generated the reports for TALY and verified them and uploaded in their team channel.

TimeEntry.Title: HBS3 sync issue to DC for Taly QNAP
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the QNAP in DC to verify the backup syncing status from Taly. It was on error state regarding the connection error. Logged into Taly QNAP at their location and then troubleshoot the issue regarding communication between RTRR server settings. Find the error and then apply fix which helps the issues resolution. After that test the connection and stat syncing again. The has been synced again properly to DC QNAP.

TimeEntry.Title: TALY-HQ-WINEDGE : Manually update patches, as APD failed to do
TimeEntry.Notes:
• Installed QNAP Finder Pro.

  • Updated Cisco Secure Client for Windows to the latest version:
    cisco-secure-client-win-5.1.14.145.

  • Confirmed successful installation and proper functionality.

  • Verified synchronization with the Cisco portal.

  • Confirmed that the MyRMM database reflects the updated version.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Release websites
TimeEntry.Notes: Released the weblink from 365 and checked with Christina from his office as he was not available. It was working fine.

TimeEntry.Title: Release websites
TimeEntry.Notes:
Release the email from cisco umbrella. websites are on global allow list. 

Robert said it didn't work, sent instruction to delete cache on the mac from using terminal. reopen the browser and try.

 Website - (Gun websites)

www.byrna.com

www.nra.org

us.glock.com

TimeEntry.Title: Release websites
TimeEntry.Notes: Installed the remote tool on Robert computer. Check the web address, 2 links were working after the allowing the domain in cisco umbrella. 3rd link was connecting to another url. Whitelisted the url in cisco umbrella. Tried to login. User will confirm tomorrow.

TimeEntry.Title: TALY Monthly Site/Network/ Security scan - March 2026
TimeEntry.Notes: As per the monthly task, verified network detective scan reports for the Taly client and downloaded and verified the reports but the O365 reports were having the issues so troubleshooted and setup it again and started the sync and waiting for the reports once generated the reports for TALY and verified them and uploaded in their team channel.

TimeEntry.Title: TALY-HQ-WINEDGE,WIN-3V0EKA0U8E8 : Cisco umbrella old version found, need to update
TimeEntry.Notes: I found an outdated version of Cisco Umbrella on the machine, accessed it, and successfully updated it to the latest version of Cisco Secure Client (win-5.1.16.194).
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Huntress Agent updated with Version 0.14.150 on TALY-HQ-WINEDGE, WIN-3V0EKA0U8E8
TimeEntry.Notes: Login into the Huntress portal, we verified that the Huntress Agent Version on the following machines on is old version 0.14.146. Connected to the machines TALY-HQ-WINEDGE, WIN-3V0EKA0U8E8 from manage engine, download and installed the latest Version 0.14.150. After that verified in the portal it is updated to latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: CrowdStrike Sensor Updated with Version 7.34.20610.0: TALY-HQ-WINEDGE
TimeEntry.Notes: Login into the CrowdStrike portal, we verified that the CrowdStrike Sensor Version on the following machines. Connected to the machines TALY-HQ-WINEDGE from manage engine, download and installed the latest Sensor Version 7.34.20610.0. After that verified in the portal it is updated to latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: Screenconnect Latest version (V 26.1.22.9573) updated on machine WIN-3V0EKA0U8E8, TALY-HQ-WINEDGE
TimeEntry.Notes: Checked and found that the computers machine WIN-3V0EKA0U8E8, TALY-HQ-WINEDGE  were running an old version of ScreenConnect. update the software from the portal to the new version V 26.1.22.9573. After updating, verify that the version on the portal has been successfully updated to the latest version. Hence Closing this ticket .

TimeEntry.Title: TALY Monthly Site/Network/ Security scan - April 2026
TimeEntry.Notes: As per the monthly task, verified network detective scan reports for the Taly client and downloaded and verified the reports and uploaded in their team channel.

TimeEntry.Title: QNAP firmware 5.2.9.3451 upgrade for TALY
TimeEntry.Notes: I logged into the QNAP system in the TALY environment and found that firmware updates were pending. Before proceeding with the update, I verified that all backup jobs had completed successfully. Since a reboot was required prior to installing the firmware, I rebooted the QNAP device, installed the updates, and then performed a second reboot to finalise the installation. After the system came back online, I verified that all shared folders were accessible. I then re-scanned the backup repository, and the scan completed successfully.

TimeEntry.Title: Technijian/Talley
TimeEntry.Notes: The latest updates were installed on your Mac as it was pending a reboot. After restarting, the system was upgraded to the latest version. We have also installed the MyRemote agent and set up the Time Machine backup, which is currently in progress. The initial backup may take some time to complete.

TimeEntry.Title: Technijian/Talley
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary:

Worked with Gurdeep on Robert’s machine setup. Installed and configured ScreenConnect for remote access.

Configured Time Machine backup for the Mac and verified backup setup.

Upgraded the QNAP datastore by increasing the storage capacity.

Status: Setup and upgrades completed successfully.
